payload.source_text: The pocket money tracker system allows children to claim pocket money tasks as they are completed throughout the week.
Parents can detail tasks that should be completed and the monetary amount earned for that task.
As a task is completed it is linked to the child that completed it.
The children should be able to see the available tasks and the tasks they have completed that week.
At the end of each week the children can see the total amount they have earned that week.
The Parents can also see a report of how much each child has earned and the tasks they completed so that they can provide the pocket money earned to each child.
At the start of the next week all tasks become available again to be claimed.
Historical task data shall be available.
The resulting system shall be able to operate on iOS 16, Android 9 and Windows 11 or later.

payload.description: Claim compensation for completed tasks
payload.justification: Signal SG915 describes the strategic function of claiming compensation as a core capability the enterprise must perform.

payload.description: Define task-based compensation structures
payload.justification: Signal SG916 articulates the strategic function of defining compensation structures as a key process the enterprise performs.

payload.description: Oversee task completion and compensation activity
payload.justification: Signal SG917 expresses oversight as a strategic function that the enterprise must provide to parents.

payload.description: Settle financial obligations
payload.justification: Signal SG917 identifies financial settlement as a key strategic function the enterprise performs to complete the compensation cycle.

payload.description: Household task requiring completion
payload.justification: Signal SG915 references household tasks as things of strategic importance - the core work items that drive the enterprise compensation model.

payload.description: Compensation claim for completed work
payload.justification: Signal SG915 establishes compensation claims as strategically important entities in the enterprise transaction flow.

payload.description: Task-based compensation structure
payload.justification: Signal SG916 identifies compensation structure as a strategically important thing that parents define and the system must maintain.

payload.description: Child user claiming compensation
payload.justification: Signal SG915 identifies children as key actors who claim compensation, representing a primary stakeholder role.

payload.description: Parent user defining compensation rules
payload.justification: Signals SG916 and SG917 identify parents as key actors who define compensation structures and exercise oversight, representing a primary stakeholder role.

payload.description: Enable children to claim compensation for completed household tasks
payload.justification: Signal SG915 explicitly establishes the core purpose of enabling child compensation claims, which is a strategic goal at the Planner level.

payload.description: Provide parents capability to define task-based compensation structures
payload.justification: Signal SG916 articulates parental capability to define compensation structure, representing a strategic goal for the enterprise system.

payload.description: Motivate child participation through task-based rewards
payload.justification: Signal SG916 identifies motivation as a key purpose of the compensation mechanism, representing a strategic objective.

payload.description: Enable parental oversight of task completion and compensation
payload.justification: Signal SG917 expresses parental oversight as a key system purpose, representing a strategic goal for governance and control.

payload.description: Facilitate financial settlement between parents and children
payload.justification: Signal SG917 identifies financial settlement as a key objective, representing a strategic goal for the enterprise transaction model.

payload.description: Task definition process allowing parents to create tasks with values
payload.justification: Signal describes a business capability framed as parents performing an action to define tasks, representing a core business process.

payload.description: Task completion claim function mediating fulfillment assertions
payload.justification: System purpose statement identifies task completion claims as a core business function, mediating the assertion of work done.

payload.description: Pocket money tracking function maintaining earnings records
payload.justification: System purpose statement explicitly identifies pocket money tracking as a primary business function for children.

payload.description: Task visibility function providing children access to available and completed tasks
payload.justification: Signal describes a conceptual visibility capability as a business function enabling children to view task status.

payload.description: Weekly earnings aggregation function calculating period totals
payload.justification: Signal describes aggregation capability as a business function that computes and delivers earnings totals.

payload.description: Parent reporting function presenting child-task-earnings views
payload.justification: Signal describes a reporting capability enabling parents to access reconciliation data, representing a business function.

payload.description: Task business entity representing assignable work items
payload.justification: Multiple signals explicitly reference 'task entities' as core data objects in the system, with clear business meaning as assignable units of work.

payload.description: Monetary value attribute associated with task entities
payload.justification: Signal explicitly describes monetary value as an attribute of task entities, representing the payment amount for completion.

payload.description: Child business entity representing young users of the system
payload.justification: Multiple signals reference 'child entities' as distinct business actors with associated data, including task completions and earnings.

payload.description: Completion relationship linking child entities to task entities
payload.justification: Signal explicitly establishes a conceptual relationship between completed tasks and children, representing task fulfillment.

payload.description: Weekly earnings total business data object
payload.justification: Signal describes aggregated earnings totals as a distinct data concept provided to children at period closure.

payload.description: Child-task-earnings relationship for payment reconciliation
payload.justification: Signal explicitly describes a three-way relationship connecting children, tasks, and earnings for reporting purposes.

payload.description: Historical task completion business record
payload.justification: Signal mandates persistence of historical completions, implying they exist as distinct archival data entities separate from current tasks.

payload.description: Weekly business cycle defining task availability periods
payload.justification: Multiple signals reference weekly boundaries and cycles as temporal organizing structures for task availability and reset.

payload.description: Weekly period closure event triggering earnings total provision
payload.justification: Signal describes period closure as a temporal event that triggers the delivery of earnings totals to children.

payload.description: Weekly cycle boundary trigger resetting task availability
payload.justification: Signal explicitly describes a temporal behavior triggered at cycle boundaries, causing task availability reset.

payload.description: Parent actor who defines tasks and reconciles payments
payload.justification: Multiple signals describe parents performing distinct business activities (defining tasks, viewing reports), establishing them as a key actor.

payload.description: Child actor who views tasks and receives earnings totals
payload.justification: Multiple signals describe children as users performing specific activities (viewing tasks, receiving totals), establishing them as a distinct business actor.

payload.description: Enable task-based pocket money management for families
payload.justification: System purpose statement articulates the overarching business goal of mediating task completion and pocket money tracking.

payload.description: Weekly temporal boundary constraint limiting task visibility scope
payload.justification: Signal describes a requirement that constrains task visibility to weekly boundaries, representing a business rule limitation.

payload.description: Historical data retention constraint requiring persistence of completion records
payload.justification: Signal mandates retention of historical data, representing a business constraint on data lifecycle management.

payload.description: Parent-child payment reconciliation principle requiring transparent earnings reporting
payload.justification: Signal describes a reporting capability for payment reconciliation, implying a business principle of transparency in financial accounting.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL CellContentItem elements present in the ledger.

payload.name: Household Contribution Accountability
payload.description: Enterprise concern for defining what household work is expected, who performs it, and establishing the obligation structure that governs completion responsibility and recognition of contribution.

payload.name: Compensation Governance
payload.description: Enterprise concern for establishing the rules, structures, and authority by which work translates into compensation entitlement, including who sets terms and what frameworks govern reward allocation.

payload.name: Compensation Entitlement
payload.description: Enterprise concern for the claims, rights, and obligations that arise when contribution occurs, including who may assert entitlement, what constitutes a valid claim, and why such claims must be honored.

payload.name: Financial Settlement Obligation
payload.description: Enterprise concern for the fulfillment of financial commitments between parties, representing the accountability to discharge compensation obligations once entitlement has been established.

payload.name: Task Stewardship
payload.description: Business responsibility for defining assignable work items with associated monetary value, governing their lifecycle from creation through completion, and maintaining visibility to appropriate participants throughout the weekly cycle.

payload.name: Earnings Accountability
payload.description: Business responsibility for calculating, reconciling, and reporting monetary obligations arising from task completion, ensuring transparent settlement between parties on defined temporal boundaries.

payload.name: Participation Entitlement
payload.description: Business responsibility for governing who may participate in task-based economic activities, defining roles and access rights of family members within the pocket money obligation framework.

payload.name: Completion Record Stewardship
payload.description: Business responsibility for preserving historical evidence of task fulfillment and completion events, ensuring auditability and compliance with retention obligations for settlement verification.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Domain elements present in the ledger.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish household tasks requiring completion.
payload.rationale: Defines the core entity (household task) that forms the basis of the accountability structure.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall oversee task completion and compensation activity.
payload.rationale: Establishes the enterprise's commitment to governance of the core household accountability processes.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable children to claim compensation.
payload.rationale: Commits the enterprise to providing the capability for child participation in the compensation structure.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable parents to define compensation rules.
payload.rationale: Commits the enterprise to providing parental authority over the compensation governance framework.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall motivate child participation through task-based rewards.
payload.rationale: Expresses a motivational policy constraint that governs the design of the accountability structure.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable parental oversight of task completion and compensation.
payload.rationale: Commits the enterprise to providing oversight capability as a governance instrument.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish a task-based compensation structure.
payload.rationale: What-column Entity CCI asserts the existence and composition of a compensation structure organized by tasks.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable parents to define task-based compensation structures.
payload.rationale: How-column function combined with Who-column actor and Why-column motivation expresses a single capability obligation: providing parents the authority and means to define compensation rules.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall recognise compensation claims arising from completed tasks.
payload.rationale: Entity assertion defining the existence and origin of compensation claims within the enterprise scope.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable children to claim compensation for completed tasks.
payload.rationale: Functional obligation derived from process (How), actor (Who), and motivation (Why) CCIs expressing the enterprise commitment to provide claim capability.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall settle financial obligations.
payload.rationale: How-column CCI expressing the enterprise's commitment to discharge financial obligations once entitlement is established.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall facilitate financial settlement between parents and children.
payload.rationale: Why-column CCI expressing the motivational constraint governing the scope and parties involved in financial settlement — constrains settlement to parent-child financial relationships.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a Task entity representing assignable work items.
payload.rationale: What-column Entity CCI naming the core business artefact; structural obligation to maintain the entity.

payload.statement: Each Task shall have an associated monetary value.
payload.rationale: What-column Attribute CCI describing essential Task composition; structural assertion of the value attribute.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a completion relationship linking child entities to Task entities.
payload.rationale: What-column Relationship CCI asserting structural association between children and Tasks.

payload.statement: The system shall make tasks claimable upon fulfillment by entitled children.
payload.rationale: How-column Function CCI describing the claim-mediation capability the system provides at the boundary; black-box affordance statement (WHAT, not HOW).

payload.statement: The system shall make available tasks and their completion status visible to children.
payload.rationale: How-column Function CCI describing the visibility capability provided to child actors; black-box affordance statement.

payload.statement: The business shall govern task lifecycle within a weekly business cycle.
payload.rationale: When-column Cycle CCI expressing the business responsibility to operate task availability within defined temporal periods.

payload.statement: The business shall reset task availability at each weekly cycle boundary.
payload.rationale: When-column Trigger CCI naming the business responsibility to refresh task state at the boundary event.

payload.statement: The business shall limit task visibility scope to the current weekly temporal boundary.
payload.rationale: Why-column Constraint CCI asserting a governance rule restricting visibility beyond the active cycle; constrains business behaviour.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a record of each child's earnings arising from task completion.
payload.rationale: The pocket money tracking function and child-task-earnings relationship together express the business responsibility to maintain persistent earnings records, establishing accountability for payment reconciliation.

payload.statement: The business shall calculate a weekly earnings total for each child based on completed tasks within the period.
payload.rationale: The weekly earnings aggregation function and period closure event together define the business obligation to produce period-based totals on a defined temporal boundary.

payload.statement: The business shall provide transparent earnings reporting to enable parent-child payment reconciliation.
payload.rationale: The reconciliation principle establishes a governance constraint requiring transparency in how earnings are reported, constraining the business behavior behind the boundary.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a weekly earnings total as a distinct business data object for each child.
payload.rationale: The weekly earnings total entity represents a structural obligation—a specific business data object the business must recognize and maintain with defined temporal scope.

payload.statement: The system shall enable task-based pocket money management for families.
payload.rationale: System-subject statement expressing the black-box capability provided to meet the business goal (Why-column informing the affordance the system must provide).

payload.statement: The business shall recognise a child entity representing young users of the system.
payload.rationale: Business-subject structural statement asserting the existence and nature of the child entity (What-column entity requiring structural definition).

payload.statement: A completed task shall be linked to the child entity who completed it.
payload.rationale: Defines the structural relationship between completion records and child entities, establishing the foundational data model for completion stewardship.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a historical record of each task completion.
payload.rationale: Business responsibility for preserving completion evidence as a persistent artefact, enabling auditability and settlement verification.

payload.statement: The business shall retain completion records for the duration required to support settlement verification and compliance obligations.
payload.rationale: Why-column retention constraint governing the persistence of historical completion data; establishes the business rule that completion records must not be prematurely deleted.

payload.statement: A parent can define tasks with values.
payload.rationale: CCI-ROW2-C-Who-001 identifies the parent actor who defines tasks; CCI-ROW2-C-How-001 describes the task definition process allowing parents to create tasks with values. These express a single boundary-crossing actor capability (Who-column) — the parent reaching into the system to perform task definition. Subject = actor; verb = their real action (define). The How-column process CCI is the enabler of this actor action and forms a complementary pair rather than a separate obligation.

payload.statement: A parent can reconcile payments.
payload.rationale: CCI-ROW2-C-Who-001 identifies the parent actor who reconciles payments. This is a distinct boundary-crossing capability separate from task definition. Atomicity test: defining tasks and reconciling payments are two distinct obligations requiring separate statements.

payload.statement: A child can view tasks.
payload.rationale: CCI-ROW2-C-Who-002 identifies the child actor who views tasks. Boundary-crossing actor capability (Who-column). Subject = actor; verb = their real action (view).

payload.statement: A child can receive earnings totals.
payload.rationale: CCI-ROW2-C-Who-002 identifies the child actor who receives earnings totals. Distinct boundary-crossing capability separate from viewing tasks. Atomicity test: viewing tasks and receiving earnings totals are two distinct obligations.

payload.statement: The system shall present child-task-earnings views to parents.
payload.rationale: CCI-ROW2-C-How-006 describes the parent reporting function presenting child-task-earnings views. This is a system-subject affordance (How-column capability) — what the system provides at the boundary to enable parent reporting, stated as a black-box capability without implementation detail.

payload.description: Establishes the core purpose enabling children to claim compensation for completed household tasks.
Articulates parental capability to define task-based compensation structure as motivational mechanism.
Expresses parental oversight and financial settlement objective as key system purpose.
Section header identifying the following content as conceptual system overview material.
Conceptual purpose statement defining the system as mediating task completion claims and pocket money tracking for children.
Conceptual capability allowing parents to define task entities with associated monetary value attributes.
Conceptual relationship establishing linkage between completed task entities and child entities.
Conceptual visibility requirement enabling children to view available and completed task entities within weekly boundaries.
Conceptual aggregation capability providing children with weekly earnings totals at period closure.
Conceptual reporting capability enabling parents to view child-task-earnings relationships for payment reconciliation.
Conceptual temporal behavior defining task availability reset at weekly cycle boundaries.
Conceptual data retention requirement mandating persistence of historical task completion entities.
